Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Activities: RO, CA, RI, PI, QC, PO, GSL, D, IC, RP typical sequence 1. Receive Order RO 2. Check Availability CA 3. Reserve Items RI 4. Pick Items PI 5. Quality Check QC 6. Pack Order PO 7. Generate Shipping Label GSL 8. Dispatch D 9. Invoice Customer IC 10. Record Payment RP

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: reason about the process flow An order starts with RO Then CA is done to check availability After CA RI is done to reserve items Then PI picks the items Then QC checks the quality Then PO packs the order Then GSL generates shipping label Then D dispatches Then IC invoices the customer Then RP records payment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: assume a straightforward process without exceptions every case contains all activities exactly once in the order RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: all activities occur exactly once so equivalence holds for all pairs of activities always_before for pairs where second precedes first in sequence always_after for pairs where first precedes second never_together empty activ_freq each activity {1}

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: directly_follows the consecutive pairs RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: generate the sets using order list and loops for i in range for j in range i+1 to generate equivalence always_before always_after

Name: Validation - PE
Text: double-check the process sequence and constraints including direct follows even with possible time gaps but no other activities so consecutive pairs hold
